business.headline: India's digital payments boom has a growing fraud problemIndia's digital boom has made payments effortless, but also easier to exploit. From fake identities to instant scams, fraudsters are moving faster than the systems meant to stop them, and users are paying the price.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

On paper, compliant: How labour laws are quietly underminedIn the third episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how employers maintain compliance on paper while systematically evading legal obligations in practice.

Digital gold, ETFs or SGBs: Where should you invest this Akshaya Tritiya?Akshaya Tritiya is here, and gold buying is once again in focus. But instead of just buying jewellery, many are now exploring smarter ways to invest. So, which option, digital gold, ETFs or SGBs, makes the most sense?
Oil prices slide over 10% as Iran opens HormuzIran's foreign minister said all commercial ships can pass through the Strait of Hormuz during the remaining ceasefire period.

Your FY27 game plan: Simplifying insurance, investments, tax and emergency fundPart 3 of our 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series explains why the start of the financial year is the right time to focus on insurance, investments, tax and emergency fund planning to guide the rest of the year.

Explained: Why India slipped to 6th-largest economy despite strong growthIndia has slipped to the 6th-largest economy in the IMF's latest outlook. Why did it happen despite strong growth? IndiaToday.in spoke to economists to find out.
Sensex ends 500 points higher as HUL leads market rally with 5% riseThe S&P BSE Sensex added 504.86 points to end at 78,493.54, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 156.80 points to close at 24,353.55.

Sebi allows 50% IPO size cut without refiling: What it means for investorsThe regulator has taken this step as market participants are finding it difficult to mobilise resources and access capital markets in the current geopolitical environment.

Akshay Tritiya property plans? Don't miss these 5 key checksAkshay Tritiya is seen as the perfect day to make big financial moves, and property is high on many buyers' lists, but before you take the plunge, are you checking the right things?
DA hike delayed: Union body urges Govt to act quicklyThe timely announcement of DA will help restore confidence and ease concerns among lakhs of central government employees and pensioners.

Netflix shares slide over 9% as co-founder Reed Hastings steps downHastings has been a key figure in shaping Netflix into what it is today. He co-founded the company and played a major role in transforming it from a DVD-by-mail service into a global streaming giant.

Forced to mis-sell: IIIT graduate quits Rs 17 LPA bank job, cites sales pressureChirag Madaan's resignation from his Rs 17 lakh per annum banking job has reignited focus on mis-selling at financial institutions, an issue IndiaToday.in had earlier highlighted through its investigation into how sales targets and incentives shape what customers are sold.

Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani to become Asia's richestThe latest ranking places Gautam Adani at the top spot in Asia, with Mukesh Ambani now in second place. Both remain among the top 25 richest people globally, even as their wealth levels have seen changes this year.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. The change says more about currency than growth, with projections suggesting the loss in ranking may not last long.

Markets open flat as geopolitical tensions ease, but caution remainsThe BSE Sensex was trading at 78,030.99, up 42.31 points or 0.05% at around 9:43 am. The Nifty 50, however, slipped marginally by 6.85 points or 0.03% to 24,189.90.

Gurugram circle rates increase by up to 75%: How much will home prices rise?The revision in Gurugram circle rates comes at a time when the city continues to see strong demand due to its proximity to the airport and the presence of major offices and industries.

Rs 8 lakh crore stuck: How delayed payments are hurting India's MSMEsAcross India, the total value of such pending payments to micro, small and medium enterprises is estimated at Rs 8.1 lakh crore, according to industry estimates and government assessments.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,178 at 8:06 am, suggesting that the Nifty 50 may open close to its previous session's level of 24,196.75. This points to a muted start for the markets after recent volatility.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 31: Film beats Pushpa 2, hits Rs 1,749 crore globally Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, starring Ranveer Singh, continues its strong box office run and has surpassed Pushpa 2 in global earnings. On Day 31, the film has hit Rs 1,749 crore worldwide, maintaining steady momentum even in its fifth weekend.

Ms Marvel's Mohan Kapur talks Hollywood, Bollywood and returning to theatre Actors Mohan Kapur and Vidya Malvade are returning to theatre with their new play Merry Go Round, set to be staged in Mumbai.

Dhurandhar actor Gaurav Gera on why he won't go back to being deeply in love again Actor Gaurav Gera admits he was deeply in love once, revealing the reason he would want to avoid going back to such a situation once again.

14-hour shifts, no lunch breaks: Archana Puran Singh slams film set work culture Archana Puran Singh raised concerns about long working hours and lack of basic facilities on film sets, calling out the impact on crew members. She highlighted how extended shifts and skipped meal breaks reflect a deeper issue in the industry's work culture.

Samay Raina says his grandfather was on a kill list, recalls Kashmiri Pandit exodus Samay Raina said his grandfather was on a hit list during the Kashmiri Pandit exodus and revealed how Kashmiri Muslims helped his family escape.

Was starting to get that image: Ali Asgar on quitting Dadi act in Kapil Sharma show Ali Asgar said he quit playing Dadi on The Kapil Sharma Show as he was starting to get offers only to play female roles. He added that he wanted to move beyond typecasting and grow as an actor.

Prakash Raj slams Pawan Kalyan over Women's Bill comment, challenges him to debate Prakash Raj has criticised actor and Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan for his remarks on the Women's Reservation Bill. Prakash Raj accused Pawan Kalyan of misleading citizens and challenged him to a public debate.

Sonali Raut claims she got scabies on Bigg Boss Marathi 6: Rats, cockroaches on show Sonali Raut says she faced hygiene issues during her stint on Bigg Boss Marathi 6. Claiming that she developed scabies, and was exposed to rats and cockroaches on the show, she has raised the issue on social media and sent a legal notice to the makers.

Akshay Kumar says son Aarav earns Rs 4,500: He is learning fashion in villages Akshay Kumar says his son Aarav Bhatia has no plans to join films. He adds that Aarav has a Rs-4,500 job and is learning fashion from the grassroots level in villages.

Vijay Varma rejects rush culture, calls himself a work in progress Vijay Varma spoke about his journey in films, saying his growth has been steady. He also reflected on his success on streaming platforms and said he prefers to enjoy the present rather than chase milestones.

Par excellence: Aditya Dhar praises Dhurandhar choreographer Vijay Ganguly Director Aditya Dhar has praised Vijay Ganguly for his choreography in the Dhurandhar films. Dhar also credited Ganguly for his dance direction in Shararat, one of the viral songs this season.

Ayushmann Khurrana's Pati Patni Aur Woh Do to release in May The makers of Pati Patni Aur Woh Do have released the first look of their upcoming comedy-drama, featuring Ayushmann Khurrana as Prajapati Pandey caught in a humorous love tangle. Set to hit theatres in May, the film promises a quirky and entertaining continuation of the 2019 hit.

Scorpions cancel India tour due to medical emergency, ticket refunds assured German rock band Scorpions have cancelled their Coming Home India tour due to unforeseen medical issues affecting the members, disappointing fans across major cities. The band expressed regret but assured ticket refunds.

Vijay Deverakonda announces his next film with Hi Nanna director Shouryuv Vijay Deverakonda has announced his next ambitious film, VDxShouryuv, collaborating with director Shouryuv. Shouryuv is best known for his hit Hi Nanna, with actors Nani and Mrunal Thakur.

Zayn Malik hospitalised ahead of UK show, says still recovering Zayn Malik's much-awaited UK concert has been postponed due to a sudden health issue. As the singer focuses on recovery, organisers are working on new dates and will share updates, including refund options, soon.

Akshay Kumar shares disappointing news for Hera Pheri 3 fans amid legal trouble With Hera Pheri 3 facing delays, Akshay Kumar has now shared a rather disappointing update on the film. Unresolved issues and legal complications have been stalling the project for quite some time now.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 1: Akshay scores his best opener since Housefull 5 Bhooth Bangla marks the reunion of Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan. While the horror-comedy has started on a steady note, it trails the genre's recent big openers and has received mixed reactions for its humour and scares.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 30: Film ties with Pushpa 2, to soon dethrone Baahubali 2 The box office race between Dhurandhar 2 and Pushpa: The Rule has tightened, with both films hovering around Rs 1,742 crore globally after a month. As Dhurandhar maintains a steady run across markets, it now faces fresh competition from Bhooth Bangla.

Ramayana, Toxic, Alpha and more: Indian cinema's global dreams and real challenges Indian films are getting bigger, the audience is expanding globally and the ambition for a worldwide hit is real. But ambition and infrastructure are not the same thing, and right now the gap between the two is hard to ignore.

general news.headline: Trump's 'favourite field marshal' Asim Munir's Iran ties a red flag for USGeneral Munir is playing a key backchannel mediator between Washington and Tehran. However, critics argue that this positioning is complicated by his reported long-standing proximity to senior figures within Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).

If they make slightest mistake, will respond with full force: Iran warns USIran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said Tehran is open to diplomacy after what he described as a battlefield victory, but warned that any misstep by adversaries would trigger a strong military response.

Opposition committed foeticide of your rights: PM to women after quota setbackPM Modi's national address comes a day after the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, aimed to increase Lok Sabha seats for fast-tracking the implementation of women's reservation, was defeated on Friday.

After Neeraj, Sumit harassment complaint, Paralympic committee sacks Naval SinghDronacharya awardee coach Naval Singh has been removed by the Paralympic Committee of India after harassment allegations by Neeraj Chopra and Paralympic gold medallist Sumit Antil.

Why Modi used crime of female foeticide to attack Opposition and what it signalsPM Narendra Modi compared the Opposition's defeating the bill to reserve seats for women to female foeticide. The use of foeticide sharpens the messaging, adds a grisly imagery, and associates the Opposition with a hideous crime.

Loyal, bold, courageous: Trump praises Israel, says others showed true coloursUS President Donald Trump on Sunday praised Israel as a "great ally of the United States," praising its courage and loyalty amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. In a post on Truth Social, Trump said that, regardless of public opinion, Israel has consistently stood by the United States.

Firing on Indian ships in Hormuz reveals who wields real power in IranMixed signals from Tehran are colliding with hard control on the ground. Two India-flagged ships were fired at in the Strait of Hormuz. This came as the IRGC pushed back hours after Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the Strait of Hormuz was open.

They got a little cute: Trump mocks Iran, says Hormuz move won't blackmail USDonald Trump mocked Iran's renewed closure of the Strait of Hormuz, saying Tehran "can't blackmail" the US, while asserting that ongoing talks are "working out really well."

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: Resolute Hyderabad bowlers defy odds to choke ChennaiSRH vs CSK, IPL 2026: If someone had told you that a team would defend a sub-200 total in Hyderabad, you would have probably laughed it off and credited them with a fine sense of humour. After all, this is a venue where bowlers often come just to survive the carnage, not control it.

Lenskart bindi-hijab storm refuses to die down despite CEO's clarification. Here's whyDespite clarifications by Lenskart CEO Peyush Bansal that the dress code was outdated and not reflective of current policy, outrage has only intensified. Calls for boycotting Lenskart are growing louder. The Lenskart document, which disallowed tilak, kalawa and sindoor while permitting hijab, has been slammed as discriminatory and anti-Hindu. Later on Saturday, Lenskart issued an apology.

Congress mentioned 59 times, women barely: Kharge slams PM's address to nationA political firestorm erupts after PM Narendra Modi's address following the women's reservation bill defeat, with Mallikarjun Kharge, Jairam Ramesh and Manish Tewari accusing him of partisan attacks, misinformation and targeting Opposition parties.

Live: US Apache helicopters patrol Hormuz as Iran reimposes restrictionsUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Fresh uncertainty grips the region as Iran says nuclear talks with the US have made progress but key gaps remain, even as Donald Trump claims "very good conversations" while warning against "blackmail" over the Strait of Hormuz. Just days before a fragile ceasefire deadline, Tehran has again closed the vital oil route after briefly reopening it, escalating tensions in the eighth week of the war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy flows. Reports of gunfire on vessels, India flagging attacks on its ships, and threats of renewed US strikes underscore the volatility, with no date set for the next round of negotiations and the risk of further escalation looming. Follow indiatoday.in for all live updates.

KV schools are topping India's boards. So why are bureaucrats opting out?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) are still outperforming most schools in the country. But while the results remain intact, the constituency they were meant to serve is quietly shifting, and that may matter more.

No date set for next round of Iran-US talks, says Iran deputy foreign ministerKhatibzadeh indicated that while some progress had been made during the first round, the discussions ultimately broke down due to what he described as a "maximalist approach" from the US side.

India summons Iran envoy over firing on Indian vessels, seeks safe Hormuz transitIndia summons Iranian envoy after Indian-flagged vessel was fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz; no injuries reported, as New Delhi raises concerns and steps up efforts to protect seafarers.

Woman killed by boss's wife over suspicion of affair in PuneThe accused has been identified as 42-year-old Swati Sawant. She allegedly killed her husband's staffer and tried to hide the body by stuffing it into a sack.

Made Ayush Mhatre run when limping! Ashwin slams CSK's poor injury managementIPL 2026, SRH vs CSK: Former spinner R Ashwin did not hold back in his critique of Chennai Super Kings, saying he was shocked to see the five-time champions let Ayush Mhatre carry on despite a hamstring injury on Saturday. Ashwin added that those in the commentary box could not believe their eyes, slamming CSK's poor injury management.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

5 killed after gunman opens fire in Kyiv, shooter killed in supermarket standoffKyiv Police units took cover inside a mall complex during the standoff as gunfire continued, with four hostages later being rescued and the attacker getting killed in the operation.

Tharoor spots silver lining as Hormuz tensions boost Kerala's Vizhinjam port trafficIn a post on X, Tharoor said nearly 100 vessels are either queued or seeking berthing at the port, signalling a shift in global maritime traffic. "The Strait of Hormuz crisis has shifted global shipping eyes to my constituency," he wrote, calling it a significant moment for India's maritime ambitions.

Thin outside, fat inside: Why your liver doesn't care if you look fitHandcrafted ByOur Editors Fatty liver doesn't come with dramatic warning signs. There's no sudden pain, no immediate red flags. It's more subtle than that, like a slow leak you ignore until the damage is done. And it all lies in our phenotype.

No rule forbids Iran: Tehran rejects EU stand on Hormuz transit rightsThe exchange comes amid heightened tensions in the region, with increased military activity raising concerns over disruptions to global shipping. The Strait of Hormuz remains one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints, handling a significant share of global oil and trade flows.

North Korea fires multiple ballistic missiles, seventh launch this yearNorth Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles off its east coast, marking its seventh launch this year and escalating regional tensions, according to South Korea and Japan.

Nearly 7 lakh new voters added in West Bengal ahead of assembly electionsAround 3.22 lakh of these new voters will vote in the first phase, while the remaining nearly 3.88 lakh voters will exercise their franchise in the second phase, a senior official of the poll body said.

sports.headline: KKR vs RR preview: Already in ruins, Eden braces for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi storm IPL 2026, KKR vs RR preview: The only winless team in the tournament, Kolkata Knight Riders brace for another stiff test as they welcome a wounded Rajasthan Royals side. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be in focus at Eden Gardens on Sunday, with the teenager keen to bounce back from a rare failure.

City vs Arsenal preview: Guardiola's final chance to win Premier League Manchester City and Arsenal meet in what could be the defining game of the Premier League title race, with Pep Guardiola calling it a "final" for City. Arsenal still lead the table, but with City holding a game in hand, Sunday's clash at the Etihad could completely shift the balance of the season.

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: Resolute Hyderabad bowlers defy odds to choke Chennai SRH vs CSK, IPL 2026: If someone had told you that a team would defend a sub-200 total in Hyderabad, you would have probably laughed it off and credited them with a fine sense of humour. After all, this is a venue where bowlers often come just to survive the carnage, not control it.

Made Ayush Mhatre run when limping! Ashwin slams CSK's poor injury management IPL 2026, SRH vs CSK: Former spinner R Ashwin did not hold back in his critique of Chennai Super Kings, saying he was shocked to see the five-time champions let Ayush Mhatre carry on despite a hamstring injury on Saturday. Ashwin added that those in the commentary box could not believe their eyes, slamming CSK's poor injury management.

What's wrong with Head? Pundits analyse SRH batter's lukewarm start to IPL Aaron Finch and Ambati Rayudu dissected the reason behind Travis Head's lacklustre start to the IPL 2026 campaign. Head has scored just 143 runs in six matches.

Yellow mellowed at Uppal as disciplined SRH snatch win from CSK in Hyderabad A disciplined SRH bowling unit fought back with a lot of pride as SunRisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings in a nervy game on Saturday. Defending 194, SRH fought till the end and restricted CSK to 184/8 in 20 overs.

After Neeraj, Sumit harassment complaint, Paralympic committee sacks Naval Singh Dronacharya awardee coach Naval Singh has been removed by the Paralympic Committee of India after harassment allegations by Neeraj Chopra and Paralympic gold medallist Sumit Antil.

Ex-Wimbledon champion faces 4-year ban after refusing doping test Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been charged by the ITIA over an alleged doping-control breach after an out-of-competition test in December. She denied any wrongdoing, saying the incident was driven by fear and anxiety amid mental health struggles.

RCB blame tacky Chinnaswamy pitch for loss, but team won't overanalyse result Royal Challengers Bengaluru suffered their first loss at home in the IPL 2026 season. Captain Rajat Patidar and director of cricket Mo Bobat pinned the loss on the tacky pitch at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium.

Matheesha Pathirana all set to join KKR. Will he play against Rajasthan Royals? IPL 2026: Matheesha Pathirana is set to join Kolkata Knight Riders after receiving post-injury clearance, but it remains to be seen if he will feature immediately, with his fitness to be assessed ahead of their clash with Rajasthan Royals.

Bhuvneshwar Kumar for India again? RCB pacer's heroics vs DC fuels comeback talks IPL 2026, RCB vs DC Ex-India batter Mohammad Kaif hailed Bhuvneshwar Kumar after his vintage spell against Delhi Capitals, saying the 36-year-old still has what it takes to return to the national side.

Emotional David Miller vanquishes last over demons to complete DC's RCB heistDavid Miller vanquished the ghosts of DC's heartbreaking loss against GT to power his side to a famous win at the Chinnaswamy stadium against RCB.

RCB v DC: Stubbs and Miller ensure KL Rahul's birthday bash is on in BengaluruIPL 2026, RCB vs DC: Delhi Capitals chased down 176 on a sluggish Bengaluru pitch on Saturday. Birthday boy KL Rahul struck a fifty before Tristan Stubbs and David Miller produced a clinical finishing act to deny Royal Challengers Bengaluru a climb to the top of the points table.

Chelsea vs Manchester United: High stakes clash as both sides look to bounce backManchester United and Chelsea meet in a high-stakes clash, with both sides looking to recover after disappointing losses last weekend. United face defensive concerns, while Chelsea are under pressure after a poor run of form and mounting scrutiny on their manager.

Why Neeraj hasn't picked up a javelin in last 6 months? Indian star revealsSpeaking to India Today's sister channel, Sports Today, Neeraj Chopra recalled why he split with Jan Zelezny after the eighth-place finish at Tokyo World Championships. Having not thrown a javelin since, the two-time Olympic medalist is preparing for the new season with caution and clarity.

No MS Dhoni vs SRH, still a sea of yellow: CSK fans take over HyderabadIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings played without MS Dhoni again, but fans still turned Hyderabad into a sea of yellow, showing unwavering support for the iconic cricketer. Despite his absence due to injury, Dhoni's massive fan following remains unmatched.

The Premier League Weekly: Can Arsenal rally their fans against Manchester City?As the Premier League season nears its end, the battle for the title intensifies with Arsenal and Manchester City facing off in a crucial matchup, while teams like Liverpool and Tottenham grapple with their own challenges.

WWE WrestleMania 42: Match card, livestreaming details and all you need to knowWrestleMania 42 is all set to capture the imagination of the fans with some big names set to compete at the event. All the major titles will be defended on the show which will take across two nights.

Virat Kohli dismissed for 19 in Bengaluru: Is the green jersey unlucky for him?IPL 2026, RCB vs DC: Virat Kohli scored 19 off 13 balls against Delhi Capitals while playing in RCB's green jersey, continuing a mixed run in the franchise's 'Green Initiative' matches. Despite his strong overall record in the kit, RCB have struggled collectively, winning only five of 14 games in the green jersey.

Djokovic, Eileen Gu to host Laureus 2026; India women's cricket team eye top prizeNovak Djokovic and Eileen Gu will host the prestigious Laureus Sports Awards at Madrid's Palacio de Cibeles on Monday, where the India women's cricket team, fresh off their ODI World Cup triumph, will be among the top contenders.

Ajinkya Rahane faces heat for KKR's lack of game awareness, lack of risks vs GTAnil Kumble slams KKR's "lack of game awareness" after a 5-wicket loss to GT. Ajinkya Rahane faces heat for tactical errors as Shubman Gill's masterclass keeps KKR winless in IPL 2026.

Sarfaraz Ahmed named red-ball head coach of Pak cricket ahead of Bangladesh seriesFormer Pakistan captain Sarfaraz Ahmed has been appointed head coach of the Test side ahead of the series against Bangladesh national cricket team, with Umar Gul and Asad Shafiq joining as bowling and batting coaches. The Pakistan Cricket Board also named a 16-member squad led by Shan Masood, including four uncapped players, for the upcoming series.

Ashish Nehra like a football coach, keeps everyone united in GT: Harbhajan SinghIPL 2026: Harbhajan Singh praised Gujarat Titans head coach Ashish Nehra for his energetic, football-coach-like style and ability to keep the team united and comfortable, crediting him as a key force behind the franchise's strong culture and success.

Rashid Khan welcomes baby boy Azlan after Gujarat Titans' win vs KKR in IPL 2026Rashid Khan announced the birth of his son Aslan in a heartfelt post, sharing a tender first glimpse, even as he played a key role in Gujarat Titans' win over KKR in the IPL.

RCB vs DC Highlights: Tristan Stubbs, David Miller power Delhi to thrilling win in BengaluruBengaluru (RCB) vs Delhi (DC) Highlights, IPL 2026: David Miller, KL Rahul and Tristan Stubbs starred as Delhi Capitals beat the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 6 wickets at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru.

A promise kept: Frank Lampard helps Coventry secure promotion to PL after 25 yearsCoventry City ended a 25-year absence from the Premier League by securing direct promotion with a dramatic late equaliser against Blackburn Rovers. The achievement marks a remarkable turnaround for the club after decades of financial struggles, lower-league football, and exile from their home stadium.

Mahi Mania in Hyderabad, CSK eye first away win against rejuvenated SRHIPL 2026, CSK vs SRH preview: Chennai Super Kings arrive in Hyderabad on the back of two consecutive wins. There is 'Dhoni mania' in the city, but it remains to be seen whether the Super Kings can halt the SunRisers, who are threatening to peak after a slow start to their season.

When Shreyas Iyer's blinder made Jonty Rhodes feel like grandfather of fieldingIPL 2026: Jonty Rhodes praised Shreyas Iyer's stunning fielding effort, joking that the modern standard has left him feeling like the 'grandfather of fielding' in comparison to today's athletic brilliance.

Congratulations after a loss? The bizarre exchange that left Ajinkya Rahane movedIPL 2026, GT vs KKR: As Ajinkya Rahane's winless KKR crumbled again in Gujarat, a rare post-match moment of empathy offered a stark emotional contrast. Rahane thanked a journalist who congratulated him for his hard work.

Brendon McCullum to rejoin KKR? Pietersen makes wild predictionIPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen predicts Brendon McCullum will return as KKR coach next season as the team remains winless in IPL 2026. Kolkata sits at the bottom of the table after a 5-wicket loss to GT.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
